what_it_does: A standalone @dsCard preview (group 'Brand') showing the 'Ink' text-colour scale — four tiers of warm near-black — as 'Aa' specimen chips with name, hex, and usage description for each.
observations: Comment header `@dsCard group="Brand" name="Ink scale" subtitle="Four tiers of warm near-black" viewport="700x200"`.
Links shared `_card.css`; card uses --bg-canvas background and tokens for fonts/colours.
Four tiers: primary #1F1A12 (body & headlines), secondary #6B5F47 (helper text), muted #A89678 (placeholders/captions), inverse #FBF7EC (on dark surfaces).
Each chip renders an 'Aa' specimen in --font-display; first three on white, the inverse tier on the #1F1A12 ink background.
Lead text: 'Ink — near-black with warm undertone, never pure black.'
standouts: Explicit rule 'never pure black' — warm undertone is a deliberate brand choice.
Pairs each colour with a concrete usage role (body, helper, placeholder, on-dark).
conventions: Hardcodes the literal hex values inline (expected for a colour-definition card) while using tokens for the surrounding chrome.
notes: A swatch card defining the four warm near-black text-colour tiers and where each is used.
questions: How do these ink hexes map to the --fg-primary/secondary/muted/inverse tokens used elsewhere in this same file?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — metadata promises four tiers of warm near-black and the body delivers exactly that with usage notes.
apparent_themes: Warm-toned ink instead of pure black
intention_signals: Aims to document the foreground/text colour ramp and enforce the warm-near-black rule, giving each tier a named role rather than a raw value.
novelty: The warm-undertone, never-pure-black ink doctrine paired with explicit per-tier usage.
contribution: Contributes the canonical text/foreground colour scale (four warm-ink tiers with roles and hexes) that a fused system would keep as its typography-colour reference.
summary_for_embedding: A Brand-group design-system preview card showing the four-tier warm near-black 'Ink' text-colour scale (primary, secondary, muted, inverse) with hex values and usage roles.
intention_for_embedding: To define the brand's foreground/text colour tiers as warm near-black and assign each a usage role.
